pages.full_text:
for refurbishment. The art is beautifully laid out. We saw Egyptian things, including an axe, Mediaeval ivories + lovely paintings. We saw St. Nizier's church + walked across a red footbridge to the Cathedral. There was an amusing picture on a restaurant window of St. George sharing a bottle of wine, seated at a table, with a dragon, + the dragon was gobbing the chicken between them.

	Danny + Fran gave us a super bbq in the evening. Eve had a bath. The boys went cycling.
	__Saturday__
	We had a relaxed breakfast. We failed to get the email to work to reach William. We left + had lunch in a park, v. modern, + then went to the Lumieres Museum + learned about early cinematography. Old silent French movies. 1895. The gardener being doused with water by the garden hose.
	After tea in the carpark we left + had much trouble finding Heyrieux, Mas de la Foret
	We had no wine at supper and at 9.30pm we left off for Lyons airport to meet Peter.
pages.notes: Page number not visible — scan begins mid-text, top of page cut off or no number present. 'gobbing' may read as 'bbqing' or 'bbq-ing' — text seems to say the dragon was 'bbqing the chicken between them' but reads most naturally as 'gobbing'. 'Lumieres Museum' is the Lumière museum in Lyon (Institut Lumière). 'Mas de la Foret' could also read 'Mas de la Forêt'. 'at supper' is underlined in the original.

pages.full_text: Eve had to drive because Simon only had his dark glasses + it was dark. It seemed a long way with lots of roundabouts, + we were all glad when we got back safely. We had cup-a-soup + there was lots of rain in the night + early morning.
photo_captions: Le Puy en Velay 650 m. St. Michel Rocher d'Aiguilhe
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in colour photograph of a rocky outcrop with a chapel/building, captioned 'Le Puy en Velay 650 m. St. Michel Rocher d'Aiguilhe' written along the right margin at an angle. Text at top continues from left page. 'Aiguilhe' may also be spelled 'Aiguilhe'.
